meta.summary: ByteDance officially launched Seed 2.0 and previewed Dola-Seed-2.0 on Arena, marking a notable new frontier-model push from one of China’s largest consumer internet platforms. OpenAI also launched its Safety Fellowship to fund independent alignment talent development, while a new robotics system, GEN-1, reported 99% task reliability on manipulation work such as box folding and vacuum repair. Separately, Zero Shot, a fund tied to OpenAI alumni, is reportedly targeting $100 million, signaling continued capital formation around the OpenAI ecosystem.

We show how to configure an agent that understands your organization’s processes, connects to your HR systems, and automates common tasks, such as answering new-hire questions and tracking document completion

items.title: Building Intelligent Search with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon OpenSearch for hybrid RAG solutions
items.summary_short: In this post, we show how to implement a generative AI agentic assistant that uses both semantic and text-based search using Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, Strands Agents and Amazon OpenSearch

items.title: VibeVoice Technical Report

items.title: Generative World Renderer
items.summary_short: Scaling generative inverse and forward rendering to real-world scenarios is bottlenecked by the limited realism and temporal coherence of existing synthetic datasets. To bridge this persistent domain gap, we introduce a large-scale, dynamic dataset curated from visually complex AAA games.

items.title: Steerable but Not Decodable: Function Vectors Operate Beyond the Logit Lens
items.summary_short: Function vectors (FVs) -- mean-difference directions extracted from in-context learning demonstrations -- can steer large language model behavior when added to the residual stream.

topic_refs.headline: Steerable but Not Decodable: Function Vectors Operate Beyond the Logit Lens

items.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
items.summary_short: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time. However, existing systems struggle because the key-value cache (KV cache) memory for each request is huge and grows and shrinks dynamically.
items.spotlight_comment: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time.

items.title: LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation
items.summary_short: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems enhance large language models (LLMs) by integrating external knowledge sources, enabling more accurate and contextually relevant responses tailored to user needs.
items.spotlight_comment: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems enhance large language models (LLMs) by integrating external knowledge sources, enabling more accurate and contextually relevant responses tailored to user needs.

items.title: VOID: Video Object and Interaction Deletion
items.summary_short: Existing video object removal methods excel at inpainting content "behind" the object and correcting appearance-level artifacts such as shadows and reflections.
items.spotlight_comment: Existing video object removal methods excel at inpainting content "behind" the object and correcting appearance-level artifacts such as shadows and reflections.

items.title: TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework
items.summary_short: Significant progress has been made in automated problem-solving using societies of agents powered by large language models (LLMs). In finance, efforts have largely focused on single-agent systems handling specific tasks or multi-agent frameworks independently gathering data.
items.spotlight_comment: Significant progress has been made in automated problem-solving using societies of agents powered by large language models (LLMs).

items.title: The Well: a Large-Scale Collection of Diverse Physics Simulations for Machine Learning
items.summary_short: Machine learning based surrogate models offer researchers powerful tools for accelerating simulation-based workflows. However, as standard datasets in this space often cover small classes of physical behavior, it can be difficult to evaluate the efficacy of new approaches.
items.spotlight_comment: Machine learning based surrogate models offer researchers powerful tools for accelerating simulation-based workflows.

items.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
items.summary_short: AI is increasingly playing a pivotal role in transforming how scientific discoveries are made. We introduce The AI Scientist-v2, an end-to-end agentic system capable of producing the first entirely AI generated peer-review-accepted workshop paper.
items.spotlight_comment: AI is increasingly playing a pivotal role in transforming how scientific discoveries are made. We introduce The AI Scientist-v2, an end-to-end agentic system capable of producing the first entirely AI generated peer-review-accepted workshop paper.

items.title: A decoder-only foundation model for time-series forecasting
items.summary_short: Motivated by recent advances in large language models for Natural Language Processing (NLP), we design a time-series foundation model for forecasting whose out-of-the-box zero-shot performance on a variety of public datasets comes close to the accuracy of state-of-the-art supervised forecasting

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance Ships Seed 2.0 and Previews Dola-Seed-2.0 on Arena
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The announcement marks a major model release from the company
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance’s official Seed 2.0 release is a concrete signal that major consumer platforms outside the U.S. are still investing aggressively in proprietary foundation models.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed officially announced Seed 2.0 through its own channels, making this a first-party model launch rather than a rumor or benchmark leak.
The company also released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, indicating it is putting the new model family into public comparative evaluation environments.
The same announcement stream included ByteDance Seed’s 2027 foundation-model campus recruiting push, suggesting the company is pairing model release cadence with long-horizon talent expansion.

evidence.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment is Now Open (Internships Included)

featured_topics.headline: OpenAI Launches the OpenAI Safety Fellowship
featured_topics.summary_short: A pilot program to support independent safety and alignment research and develop the next generation of talent
featured_topics.why_it_matters: This informs research and talent strategy: OpenAI is formalizing a pipeline for independent safety and alignment work rather than relying only on internal teams.
key_takeaways: OpenAI described the Safety Fellowship as a pilot program focused on supporting independent safety and alignment research.
The program is explicitly aimed at developing the next generation of safety talent, which matters for labs, universities, and nonprofits competing for the same researchers.
Because the announcement came directly from OpenAI, it is a verified strategic initiative rather than a speculative hiring signal.

evidence.title: Announcing the OpenAI Safety Fellowship

featured_topics.headline: GEN-1 Robotics Model Reports 99% Reliability on Real-World Manipulation Tasks
featured_topics.summary_short: A robotics model called GEN-1 reportedly achieved 99% reliability on tasks such as folding boxes and fixing vacuums. The report says the model can handle disruptions and infer actions beyond its explicit training examples
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If the 99% reliability figure holds up, robotics buyers and investors should pay attention to embodied AI systems that can handle disruptions and out-of-distribution actions.
key_takeaways: GEN-1 was reported to achieve 99% reliability on tasks including folding boxes and fixing vacuums, a notably concrete operational metric for robotics performance.
The system was described as able to respond to disruptions and infer moves it was not explicitly trained on, pointing to stronger generalization than narrow scripted automation.
The artifact here is a named robotics model with a quantified reliability claim, which is more decision-useful than generic humanoid robotics demos.

evidence.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability

featured_topics.headline: Zero Shot Targets a $100M Debut Fund Backed by OpenAI Alumni Network
featured_topics.summary_short: TechCrunch reports that Zero Shot, a venture capital fund with ties to OpenAI alumni, is seeking to raise a first fund of up to $100 million. The report says the fund has already made some investments
featured_topics.why_it_matters: A new $100 million AI-focused fund tied to former OpenAI personnel is a meaningful capital-allocation signal for founders, co-investors, and acquirers tracking the next startup wave.
key_takeaways: TechCrunch reported that Zero Shot is aiming to raise $100 million for its first fund, giving the market a concrete number rather than a vague emerging-manager story.
The fund reportedly has deep ties to OpenAI alumni and has already begun writing checks, suggesting deployment is underway before a final close.
This is strategically relevant because ex-OpenAI networks are increasingly becoming a distribution channel for early-stage AI company formation and financing.

evidence.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
